mission.title: keda: Provide support for explicitly pausing autoscaling of workloads.
mission.description: Provide support for explicitly pausing autoscaling of workloads.. This issue affects 36+ users.

steps.title: Identify keda feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your keda deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=keda
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Provide support for explicitly pausing autoscaling of workloads.

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
Provide support for explicitly stating workloads to scale to zero without the option of scaling up.

This can be useful for manually scaling-to-zero instances because:
- You want to do maintenance
- Y

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide support for explicitly pausing autoscaling of worklo
steps.description:
> do you want to explicitly set a replica count through this new field/CRD. I personally don't really see the need for this last option, as it's just replicating native functionality.

This is already possible today by aligning min/max replica count

> I guess the main question is: do you just want 
```yaml
apiVersion: keda.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ScaledObject
metadata:
  labels:
    deploymentName: myapp
  name: myapp
  ...
spec:
  cooldownPeriod: 600
  maxReplicaCount: 8
  minReplicaCount: 0
  pollingInterval: 30
  scaleType: deployment
  triggers:
  ...
```

steps.title: Confirm Provide support for explicitly pausing autoscaling is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: > do you want to explicitly set a replica count through this new field/CRD. I personally don't really see the need for this last option, as it's just replicating native functionality.

This is already possible today by aligning min/max replica count

> I guess the main question is: do you just want to be able to "suspend" keda scaling for a while and take manual control (with a HPA or just scaling. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: keda.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ScaledObject
metadata:
  labels:
    deploymentName: myapp
  name: myapp
  ...
spec:
  cooldownPeriod: 600
  maxReplicaCount: 8
  minReplicaCount: 0
  pollingInterval: 30
  scaleType: deployment
  triggers:
  ...

0s   Normal    ScalingReplicaSet    deployment/myapp                  Scaled up replica set myapp-5cbd86475b to 3
0s   Normal    SuccessfulCreate     replicaset/myapp-5cbd86475b       Created pod: myapp-5cbd86475b-wxjbj
0s   Normal    SuccessfulCreate     replicaset/myapp-5cbd86475b       Created pod: myapp-5cbd86475b-257k4
0s   Normal    SuccessfulRescale    horizontalpodautoscaler/myapp     New size: 1; reason: Current number of replicas above Spec.MaxReplicas
0s   Normal    ScalingReplicaSet    deployment/myapp                  Scaled down replica set myapp-5cbd86475b to 1
0s   Normal    SuccessfulDelete     replicaset/myapp-5cbd86475b       Deleted pod: myapp-5cbd86475b-257k4
0s   Normal    SuccessfulDelete     replicaset/myapp-5cbd86475b       Deleted pod: myapp-5cbd86475b-wxjbj

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
